sanskrit:
आयुः-सत्त्व-बलारोग्य-सुख-प्रीति-विवर्धनाः।
रस्याः स्निग्धाः स्थिरा हृद्या आहाराः सात्त्विकप्रियाः॥
transliteration:
āyuḥ-sattva-balārogya-sukha-prīti-vivardhanāḥ
rasyāḥ snigdhāḥ sthirā hṛdyā āhārāḥ sāttvikapriyāḥ
literal_meaning: Foods that promote life, vitality, strength, health, happiness and satisfaction; which are juicy, unctuous, nourishing and agreeable, are dear to the sattvic person.
contextual_meaning: What we consume—physically and psychologically—either stabilizes or destabilizes the mind. Sattvic choices cultivate clarity, emotional regulation and resilience, forming the dietary basis for a non-reactive, compassionate self-structure.
psychological_state: Secure, low-noise nervous system; capacity for reflective rather than reflexive action; internalized sense of sufficiency.
trigger_scenario: Choosing a fresh, home-cooked vegetarian meal over fast food during a lunch break when stressed.
anchor_text: Choose foods that nourish body and mind, fostering calm energy and gentle joy.
